name: Adarramandi Circuit
name_original: Adarra y Aballarri desde Besabi — circular por el PR-GI 203
description: Hike the difficult 4.7-mile Adarra and Aballarri circular route from Besabi, gaining 1526 feet with panoramic views and megalithic sites.
region_intro: Hiking around Urnieta offers diverse terrain characterized by mountainous landscapes, river valleys, and ancient historical sites. The region is dominated by peaks like Adarra Mountain, reaching over 800 meters, alongside the Oria River. Trails often traverse wooded canyons, open meadows, and areas featuring prehistoric monuments such as dolmens and cromlechs. This combination provides a varied natural and cultural setting for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Urnieta The most popular hiking route is Adarramandi Circuit, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 13 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Adarra Summit (849 m) – Arleorko Zabala Cromlech loop from Aballarri, a difficult 3.6 miles (5.9 km) path. This route leads to the summit of Adarra and passes ancient cromlechs. Local hikers also love the Onddi Hill – Muniskue Stone Circle loop from Aballarri, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain including Onddi Hill and a stone circle, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Urnieta is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and prehistoric monuments. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Urnieta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Urnieta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Urnieta?
A: Hiking around Urnieta offers a diverse landscape, from mountainous terrain dominated by peaks like Adarra Mountain to serene river valleys along the Oria River. Trails often lead through wooded canyons and open meadows, providing varied scenery for your outdoor adventures.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Urnieta?
A: Yes, Urnieta offers a few easier options. While many trails involve significant elevation, there are 2 routes classified as easy. For a moderate but accessible option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3992572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Onddi Hill – Muniskue Stone Circle loop from Aballarri&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.6 km long and takes about 2 hours 15 minutes.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Urnieta?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails around Urnieta are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037599029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adarramandi Circuit&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.5 km route that offers a challenging loop with significant elevation gain.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while hiking in Urnieta?
A: Urnieta's surroundings are rich in history and natural beauty. You can encounter prehistoric monuments like dolmens and cromlechs integrated into the landscape. The region is also part of the historic Way of St. James. For natural landmarks, the prominent &lt;strong&gt;Adarra Mountain&lt;/strong&gt; offers stunning panoramic views. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1338498" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adarra Summit (849 m)&lt;/a&gt; itself, or visit the ancient cromlechs on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15007088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adarra Summit (849 m) – Arleorko Zabala Cromlech loop from Aballarri&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic scenery on the trails?
A: Yes, several hikes in the area lead to excellent viewpoints. Ascents to higher elevations, particularly around Adarra Mountain, promise beautiful panoramic views of the surrounding region. The hermitage of Santa Cruz in the Azkorte neighborhood is also known for its excellent vistas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Urnieta?
A: The hiking trails in Urnieta are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the blend of natural beauty with historical sites, and the rewarding views from the summits.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes available?
A: For those seeking a more strenuous challenge, Urnieta offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24724025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adarra Summit (849 m) – Onddi Hill loop from Aballarri&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 12.8 km trail with over 700 meters of elevation gain, providing a significant workout and expansive views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Urnieta?
A: The Basque Country generally offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter might bring cooler weather and potentially snow on higher peaks like Adarra, offering a different kind of challenge.

Q: Are there any attractions near Urnieta that I can visit after a hike?
A: Urnieta is conveniently located near several attractions. Just 10 kilometers away is Donostia-San Sebastián, where you can visit landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/567533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Concha Beach&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5577591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Sebastián City Hall&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/567551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kursaal Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. For natural sights, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1041337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Monte Urgull&lt;/a&gt; offers another spectacular panorama.

Q: How many hiking routes are there in total around Urnieta?
A: There are approximately 30 hiking routes available around Urnieta, catering to various skill levels. This includes 2 easy routes, 17 moderate, and 11 difficult trails, ensuring options for almost every hiker.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by ancient stone circles or prehistoric sites?
A: Yes, the region around Urnieta is notable for its prehistoric monuments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3992572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Onddi Hill – Muniskue Stone Circle loop from Aballarri&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads you through varied terrain and past a stone circle, offering a unique blend of nature and history.
tips: Striking and repeatedly photographed prehistoric complex that stands out among the multiple sites found on Mount Adarra.

Mountain located 10 km south of San Sebastián, 811 meters high. In its surroundings, various megalithic monuments stand out such as cromlechs, dolmens, tombs and menhirs, the Eteneta cromlechs with their large menhir standing out.
Rocky summit to the north of the top of Adarra, forming part of the mountain range called Adarra/Mandoegi.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Adarra and Aballarri circular hike?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as difficult and requires good physical condition, sure-footedness, and sturdy hiking shoes. Some sections are technical, demanding, and may involve hazardous terrain, particularly near the summit of Adarra. Prior alpine experience is recommended.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Adarra and Aballarri circular trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter unpaved paths, forest sections, and grassy slopes. The ascent from Besabi starts on a cement-paved track before transitioning to a beaten mud trail through the forest. Near the Adarra Summit, expect karst terrain with heaps of stone. Aballarri Summit is known as a spectacular rocky peak. Be prepared for potentially muddy sections, especially after rain.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers breathtaking panoramic views from both the Adarra Summit and Aballarri Summit. You'll also discover a rich collection of megalithic monuments, including numerous cromlechs, dolmens, and menhirs. The Eteneta Stone Circle is a notable highlight. The landscape features indigenous forests, pastures with sheep and horses, and remnants of old snow pits.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Adarra and Aballarri circular hike from Besabi?
faqs.answer: The hike typically starts from Besabi. Parking information for Besabi is often available locally, but it's advisable to check for specific parking areas and capacity before your visit, especially on weekends or holidays.

faqs.question: Is the PR-GI 203 trail to Adarra and Aballarri dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific rules regarding dogs (leash requirements, waste disposal) may apply. It's always best to check local regulations for the Adarra-Mandoegi massif before bringing your dog, especially given the presence of livestock in pastures.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Adarra and Aballarri circular route?
faqs.answer: The summits offer expansive views, making it a popular destination for mountaineers. While it's a tradition to climb Adarra on January 1st, generally, spring and autumn provide more moderate temperatures and clearer conditions for hiking. Be aware that some sections can be muddy after rain, so consider recent weather when planning your trip.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to hike the Adarra and Aballarri circular route via the PR-GI 203. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Adarramandi inguruko ibilbidea, E03 Mendibai ibilbidea: Tolosa - Ereñotzu, and various segments of the Goiburu auzoa and Adarra bidea trails.

faqs.question: Are there any unique historical or cultural aspects to this trail?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The Adarra-Mandoegi massif boasts the most significant collection of prehistoric funerary monuments in Euskadi, with over fifty cromlechs, dolmens, and menhirs along its ridges. The Eteneta Stone Circle is particularly notable. Additionally, it's a local tradition to climb Adarra on January 1st to celebrate the New Year.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead in Besabi?
faqs.answer: While Besabi is the starting point, direct public transport options might be limited. It's recommended to research local bus services or other transport links from nearby towns like Urnieta or Donostialdea to Besabi before planning your trip.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this difficult hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, appropriate clothing layers for changing weather, plenty of water, snacks, a map or GPS device (like komoot), and potentially trekking poles for stability on technical sections. Sun protection and rain gear are also advisable.
